title: Faultsense JSON Spec Entry
description: A single JSON-spec entry — mirrors the fs-* attribute model 1:1, with an additional fs-target key naming the CSS selector the trigger binds to.

fs-target.description: CSS selector that resolves to the trigger-bearing element. JSON-only — has no HTML counterpart.

fs-assert.description: Assertion key. Used by the collector to identify the feature being asserted.

fs-assert-added.description: Pass when a matching element appears in the DOM.

fs-assert-removed.description: Pass when a matching element is removed from the DOM.

fs-assert-updated.description: Pass when a matching element or its subtree mutates.

fs-assert-visible.description: Pass when a matching element exists and is visible.

fs-assert-hidden.description: Pass when a matching element exists but is hidden.

fs-assert-stable.description: Pass when an element is NOT mutated within the timeout window.

fs-assert-emitted.description: Pass when a matching CustomEvent fires on the document.

fs-assert-route.description: Pass when the URL matches a route pattern.

fs-assert-after.description: Pass when a parent assertion (identified by its key) has already passed.

fs-assert-mpa.description: Persist the assertion across page navigation. Truthy strings enable; omit or 'false' disables.

fs-assert-timeout.description: Custom SLA timeout in milliseconds. Numeric string.

fs-assert-mutex.description: Conditional mutex mode: 'type', 'each', 'conditions', or a comma-separated list of condition keys.

fs-assert-oob.description: Out-of-band: fire this assertion when the named parent assertion passes.

fs-assert-oob-fail.description: Out-of-band: fire this assertion when the named parent assertion fails.

^fs-assert-(added|removed|updated|visibl.description: Conditional dynamic type: fs-assert-{type}-{conditionKey}. Condition keys are freeform lowercase alphanumeric strings with hyphens.
